edition.dagbeeld: De oorlog in het Midden-Oosten domineert opnieuw: olieprijs schiet door de $98 na Houthi-aanvallen op Saoedische tankers, terwijl Washington en Teheran diplomatiek blijven schaaken. Tegelijk neemt de techregulering aan beide kanten van de Atlantische Oceaan scherpte aan: de EU beboet Google met bijna een miljard euro, terwijl de controverse over AI-veiligheid en surveillancetechnologie aanhoudt.

article.title: Oil prices leap after attacks in Red Sea
article.summary: The price of Brent crude surged past $98 a barrel after Iran’s Houthi allies in Yemen claimed strikes on Saudi tankers. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the Houthis were being “suckered” by Iran into joining their fight.

opening.note: Olieprijs sprintt naar $98 na eerste Houthi-aanvallen op Saoedische tankers.
opening.label: breaking

article.title: Trump threatens Iran with escalation, including strikes on civilian and nuclear infrastructure
article.summary: After two weeks of strikes on Iran that yielded no progress, US President Donald Trump raised the possibility of targeting uranium enrichment sites beneath the Kolang mountain in central Iran.

article.title: U.S.-Saudi Deal Prompts Fears of a Nuclear Arms Race

article.title: “This price was set by an algorithm”: News subscribers are surprised by a new line in their renewal emails
article.summary: Last December, one of our readers received a renewal email from The Wall Street Journal. Over the coming year, the email said, she would be charged $76.99 every four weeks for her print and digital subscription — $19.25 a week, or $923.88 annually.
